newListings.name: Vision AI Robotic Arm
newListings.description: Vision-guided pick-and-place demo pairing the TRIA Vision AI Kit (QCS6490) with a Hiwonder xArm robotic arm. On-device object detection on the Qualcomm NPU drives the arm in real time while results and control telemetry stream to /IOTCONNECT.

newListings.name: STM32 Amazon Sidewalk Examples
newListings.description: Example projects for onboarding STM32WL Amazon Sidewalk devices to /IOTCONNECT. Demonstrates connecting a single unified Sidewalk device over sub-GHz LoRa and BLE and streaming telemetry to the cloud.

newListings.name: PSOC Edge AI Vision WebRTC
newListings.description: DEEPCRAFT AI Vision demo on the Infineon PSOC Edge E84 that streams live camera and inference results over WebRTC. Built with ModusToolbox and integrated with /IOTCONNECT for cloud monitoring and control.

newListings.name: PSOC Edge Motion Sensing
newListings.description: DEEPCRAFT motion and IMU demo for the Infineon PSOC Edge E84 that classifies movement patterns locally and reports events to /IOTCONNECT. A ModusToolbox starting point for gesture and activity-aware applications.

newListings.name: PSOC Edge DEEPCRAFT Ready Models
newListings.description: Runs Infineon DEEPCRAFT Ready Models on the PSOC Edge E84 to detect audio and sensor events out of the box. Integrated with /IOTCONNECT for live event visualization and device management.

newListings.name: PSOC Edge Secure PSA with TF-M
newListings.description: Secure PSOC Edge E84 demo running DEEPCRAFT ready models inside a PSA-certified Trusted Firmware-M (TF-M) environment. Shows how to combine hardware-backed security with on-device AI while connected to /IOTCONNECT.

newListings.name: PSOC Edge Voice Assistant
newListings.description: DEEPCRAFT voice assistant demo on the Infineon PSOC Edge E84 performing local wake-word and command recognition. Recognized events are reported to /IOTCONNECT for cloud-side automation.

newListings.name: PSOC Edge Baby Monitor
newListings.description: DEEPCRAFT baby monitor demo on the PSOC Edge E84 that detects infant cries and other sounds locally and alerts /IOTCONNECT. A smart-home audio AI reference built with ModusToolbox.

newListings.name: PSOC AI-Kit Human Activity Detection
newListings.description: Imagimob human activity detection on the PSOC 6 AI Kit, classifying movements from the on-board IMU. Detected activities are streamed to /IOTCONNECT using the ModusToolbox machine-learning flow.

newListings.name: PSOC AI-Kit Baby Monitor
newListings.description: Imagimob sound-classification baby monitor on the PSOC 6 AI Kit that recognizes infant cries from the on-board microphone. Alerts and audio-event telemetry are sent to /IOTCONNECT.

newListings.name: ModusToolbox Basic Example
newListings.description: Minimal ModusToolbox starter that connects an Infineon PSOC 6 Wi-Fi board to /IOTCONNECT. A clean baseline for sending telemetry and handling cloud commands before adding application logic.

newListings.name: Home Assistant Bridge
newListings.description: Bridges a Home Assistant instance to /IOTCONNECT over MQTT, exposing smart-home entities as cloud telemetry and accepting commands back. Lets existing Home Assistant deployments gain /IOTCONNECT dashboards and rules.

newListings.name: IoTConnect Relay Service
newListings.description: Lightweight relay that plugs existing applications into /IOTCONNECT without rewriting them. Forwards local data to the cloud and routes commands back, simplifying brownfield integrations.

newListings.name: MCX Zephyr Demos
newListings.description: Zephyr RTOS example applications connecting the NXP FRDM-MCXN947 to /IOTCONNECT. Demonstrates telemetry, commands and a Zephyr-based development workflow for NXP MCX MCUs.

newListings.name: MaaXBoard OSM93 Demos
newListings.description: Demo applications for the TRIA MaaXBoard OSM93 connecting the i.MX 93 SBC to /IOTCONNECT. Showcases edge processing and cloud telemetry on a compact embedded Linux platform.

newListings.name: Python Lite SDK Demos
newListings.boards: FRDM-IMX93, STM32MP135F-DK, ATSAMA5D27-SOM1-EK1, CURIOSITY-PIC64GX1000-KIT, MPFS-DISCO-KIT, EV63J76A, AES-MAAXB-OSM93-DK-G
newListings.description: A growing collection of /IOTCONNECT Python Lite SDK demos spanning many embedded Linux platforms from NXP, ST, Microchip and TRIA. Each example shows how to push telemetry and handle cloud commands on its target board.

newListings.name: Arduino Uno Q App Lab
newListings.description: Hands-on application lab for connecting the Arduino Uno Q to /IOTCONNECT. Guides developers through building cloud-connected sketches and Python apps with telemetry and command handling.

newListings.name: /IOTCONNECT Greengrass C Component SDK
newListings.description: C SDK for building AWS IoT Greengrass components that integrate with /IOTCONNECT. Provides native-code building blocks for telemetry, command handling and edge processing on Greengrass gateways.

newListings.name: /IOTCONNECT Python Greengrass Components
newListings.description: Ready-to-deploy AWS IoT Greengrass components written in Python for /IOTCONNECT. Reusable building blocks that send telemetry, receive commands and extend Greengrass edge deployments.

newListings.name: X-CUBE ST67 Wi-Fi SDK
newListings.description: /IOTCONNECT SDK for the STMicroelectronics ST67 Wi-Fi module, packaged as an STM32Cube expansion. Connects STM32 designs to the cloud over Wi-Fi with telemetry and command support.

newListings.name: /IOTCONNECT Node.js SDK
newListings.description: Node.js SDK for connecting JavaScript and TypeScript applications to /IOTCONNECT. Handles device authentication, telemetry publishing and command callbacks for server and gateway apps.

newListings.name: /IOTCONNECT .NET SDK
newListings.description: .NET SDK for integrating C# applications and services with /IOTCONNECT. Provides device connectivity, telemetry and command handling for Windows and cross-platform .NET deployments.

newListings.name: /IOTCONNECT iOS Swift SDK
newListings.description: Swift SDK for connecting native iOS applications to /IOTCONNECT. Enables mobile apps to act as device clients or gateways, publishing telemetry and responding to cloud commands.

newListings.name: /IOTCONNECT Android SDK
newListings.description: Android SDK for connecting Java and Kotlin apps to /IOTCONNECT. Lets mobile devices serve as edge clients or BLE gateways, streaming telemetry and handling commands.

newListings.name: /IOTCONNECT Generic C SDK
newListings.description: Portable generic C SDK for /IOTCONNECT designed to run across a wide range of platforms and RTOSes. A flexible foundation for bringing custom C-based devices online with telemetry, commands and OTA.

newListings.name: /IOTCONNECT Yocto C SDK
newListings.description: C SDK packaged for Yocto-based embedded Linux builds connecting to /IOTCONNECT. Integrates into custom Linux images to provide native telemetry and command handling on MPU platforms.

newListings.name: /IOTCONNECT Yocto Python SDK
newListings.description: Python SDK packaged for Yocto-based embedded Linux distributions connecting to /IOTCONNECT. Simplifies adding cloud telemetry and command support to custom Linux images on MPU platforms.

boardRefFixes.reason: DK3 is a typo/variant of the cataloged STM32MP157 Discovery Kit, whose canonical Part Number is STM32MP157F-DK2.
Free-text 'Raspberry Pi 5' maps to the existing Raspberry Pi5 4GB board row with Part Number RPI5-4GB-SINGLE.
Seeed reComputer J4021 is a Jetson Orin NX 16GB device; the only existing Seeed board (SEEED-j2021) is Xavier NX, so a new board row is needed.
Generic 'Kerlink Gateway' resolves to Kerlink's flagship outdoor LoRaWAN gateway, the Wirnet iStation; no Kerlink board exists yet.
Advantech SOM-6873 AMD Ryzen Embedded 8000 COM Express Type 6 module is not in the catalog and needs a new board row.
The WBZ350 module is the existing 'PIC32CX-BZ3 and WBZ35x Curiosity Board' (EV19J06A), whose product link already points at the WBZ350PE product page.
The Avnet /IOTCONNECT mobile gateway app (repo iotc-gateway-mobile-app) is a virtual gateway 'board' used by two listings and has no board row.
TE Connectivity mikroBUS click sensor boards used by the WFI-32 clicks demo are not cataloged; added as a virtual click-board family entry.
Amphenol mikroBUS click sensor boards used by the WFI-32 clicks demo are not cataloged; added as a virtual click-board family entry.

flagged.problem: Wrong board: row is the NXP MaaXBoard RT 'buy' link for boardPartNumber AES-MC-SBC-IMXRT1176-G, but the URL points to a Microchip WFI32 product (ev36w50a / 3074457345653385127). The part number, manufacturer path (microchip), and Avnet product ID all belong to a different board (the same EV36W50A used in the Microchip section). Mismatched/incorrect buy URL (propagated from the NXP README).

Wrong board: this is the 'buy' link for boardPartNumber US159-DA16600EVZ (a Pmod evaluation board), but the URL points to the DA16600MOD-DEVKT product (da16600mod-devkt / 3074457345645205670), a different SKU. The US159-DA16600EVZ has its own part number; the buy link is duplicated from the DA16600MOD-DEVKT row.

Wrong board: this row is titled 'Purchase the Infineon PSOC Edge E84 Eval Kit (Newark)' for boardPartNumber KIT_PSE84_EVAL, but the URL uses the AI Kit's slug and part number (kitpse84aitobo1, dp/49AM4459). The Eval kit is kitpse84evaltobo1 / dp/49AM4460. The buy link points at the wrong (AI) kit.

Malformed URL: the YouTube video ID 'mSETqaqMejQEach' is not a valid 11-character ID; it appears to have a corrupted/garbled 'Each' suffix appended. Likely a broken/fabricated-looking video link (also present verbatim in the Renesas README).

Malformed/non-production URL: the 'buy' link for DA16200MOD-DEVKT uses the host 'onesite-preprod.avnet.com', a preprod/staging domain rather than the public www.avnet.com store. Looks like a placeholder/staging link that leaked into the catalog (matches the Renesas README, which has the same staging host).

resources.verdict: Reviewed all rows against the five partner READMEs under C:/dev/website/avnet-iotconnect.github.io/partners/. Most rows match their READMEs and are fine, including the empty-boardPartNumber rows (Portenta H7, PSOC 62S1/62S2 kits, and demo/template/workshop entries) which legitimately have no single part number. Flagged 5 genuine problems: 3 'buy' links pointing at the wrong board/SKU (AES-MC-SBC-IMXRT1176-G -> Microchip WFI32 page; US159-DA16600EVZ -> DA16600MOD-DEVKT SKU; KIT_PSE84_EVAL Newark link -> the AI kit's kitpse84aitobo1/49AM4459 instead of the Eval kit kitpse84evaltobo1/49AM4460), 1 malformed YouTube URL (mSETqaqMejQEach has a garbled trailing 'Each'), and 1 non-production preprod/staging host (onesite-preprod.avnet.com) used as a buy link. Note: all five errors are faithfully propagated from the source READMEs, so they are upstream data-quality issues rather than fabrications introduced during harvest. The minor NUCLEO-WL55JC vs WL55JC1 and CK-RK65N vs CK-RX65N naming typos also originate in the READMEs and resolve to the correct products, so they were not flagged as hard errors.

issues.item: newListings: "Arduino Uno Q App Lab" (repo iotc-arduino-uno-q-app-lab)
issues.problem: The listing's name and description are specifically about connecting the Arduino Uno Q, but its boards field is left blank. The Arduino Uno Q is not in _wb_dump.json Boards (only Arduino OPTA RS485 / AFX00001 exists) and was not added in the newBoards set, so the card will render with no associated hardware. This is not a hard catalog violation (blank boards is permitted, and it correctly avoids a dangling reference), but it is an inconsistency for a board-specific 'sample'.
issues.suggestedFix: Either add a new board row for the Arduino Uno Q (e.g. Manufacturer 'Arduino', a real Part Number) and reference it in the boards field, or, if intentionally hardware-agnostic, leave boards blank but consider reclassifying. Recommend adding the Arduino Uno Q board so the sample links to its target hardware like the other board-specific samples.

data.verdict: APPROVE (with one minor consistency note). All reconciliation decisions are sound against C:/dev/website/iotc-index/_wb_dump.json. Verified: (1) All three rename targets exist as canonical Part Numbers — STM32MP157F-DK2 (line 1395), RPI5-4GB-SINGLE (line 1186), EV19J06A (line 1043, the 'PIC32CX-BZ3 and WBZ35x Curiosity Board' whose product link is .../product/wbz350pe, confirming the WBZ350PE-I mapping). (2) None of the six new board Part Numbers (J4021, WIRNET-ISTATION, SOM-6873, IOTC-BRIDGE-APP, TE-MIKROBUS-CLICKS, AMPHENOL-MIKROBUS-CLICKS) duplicate an existing PN. (3) The J4021 addBoard decision is correct and NOT a duplicate: J4021 appears only inside a Listings boards field (line 238, 'Edge AI Inference Pipeline'), never as a Board Part Number, and the lone existing Seeed board SEEED-j2021 is a Xavier NX device, distinct from the Orin NX reComputer J4021. (4) Every board token in all 29 new listings resolves to an existing Part Number or one of the new boards (AES-QCS6490-DK-G, NUCLEO-WL55JC, KIT_PSE84_EVAL, KITPSE84AITOBO1, CY8CKIT-062S2-AI, CY8CKIT-062-WIFI-BT, CY8CPROTO-062-4343W, FRDM-MCXN947, AES-MAAXB-OSM93-DK-G, FRDM-IMX93, STM32MP135F-DK, ATSAMA5D27-SOM1-EK1, CURIOSITY-PIC64GX1000-KIT, MPFS-DISCO-KIT, EV63J76A — all FOUND). (5) Each bad ref is genuinely present in an existing listing and the stated reason holds (Kerlink Gateway in the LoRaWAN listing line 466; TE Clicks/Amphenol Clicks in WFI-32 line 790; IOTC Bridge App in exactly two listings, lines 646 and 754; STM32MP157F-DK3 in the Nano Edge AI listing line 514 whose description says 'STM32MP157F Discovery Kit'). (6) Listing types are reasonable (samples are demos/examples; sdk entries are SDK repos). (7) Repo links follow the avnet-iotconnect org pattern and the one locally checkable repo (iotc-tria-vision-ai-kit-robotic-arm) exists and is Python-based, matching the Vision AI Robotic Arm listing — no fabricated links detected. The only item flagged is a minor consistency note below, not a blocking error.
